mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in 55% of instances where UNRATE surpasses 6.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with expanding industrial production confirms synchronized demand and supply growth, which forces equity prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in 65% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, accelerating consumer prices force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in 70% of high-inflation trigger events, or the mechanism reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate sudden labor market weakness that immediately reduces consumer spending expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of payroll decline events, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for macroeconomic surprise indices.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate profit growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or industrial production growth fails to correlate with earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts alongside robust real GDP growth confirm durable consumer demand and economic resilience, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in 55% of triggered periods, or housing data loses predictive relevance when GDP growth falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A policy pause combined with rising unemployment signals a lagging economy where monetary support fails to offset labor deterioration, generating negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains unchanged for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in 65% of pause-plus-unemployment-rising scenarios, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.
